---
title: "Data-Driven Transport Planning Hackathon"
---

Welcome to the **Data-Driven Transport Planning Hackathon**! 

Presented by the team behind the **Propensity to Cycle Tool for Northern Ireland (PCTNI)**, this afternoon session is an open-ended challenge to develop data analysis, visualisation, or prototype tool ideas that help make transport planning more data-driven.

## The Challenge

Develop a tool, visualization, or analysis that addresses a transport planning problem. While we provide datasets from the PCTNI project, your hackathon project does **not** have to be focused on cycling. 

**Ideas for inspiration:**
- High-resolution accessibility mapping.
- Visualizing multimodal origin-destination flows.
- Predicting the impact of new transport infrastructure.
- Identifying gaps in existing data.

## Participation
- Work individually or in teams.
- Use any tools or languages (R, Python, JavaScript, etc.).
- Focus on reproducibility and clear communication.

## Agenda (Monday 18th May)

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 14:00 - 14:45 | Getting started with the data and challenge |
| 14:45 - 15:00 | Break |
| 15:00 - 16:30 | **Hackathon Session** |
| 16:30 - 17:00 | Presentation of results |

## Datasets
Participants will be given access to datasets including:
- Origin-destination (OD) data for Northern Ireland.
- Zone-level demographics and transport stats.
- Simplified route networks.

See the [Prerequisites](prerequisites.qmd) page to get ready!
